judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports correctly identify all three root causes and fixes with specific files; they are comparable, with Report 1 slightly cleaner on the two-role separation but Report 2 adding a useful FORCE RLS belt-and-suspenders note.

report_1.evidence: `cedar_owner` is the schema-owning role ... Postgres table owners (and any role that created a table) are not subject to their own row-level-security policies unless `FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` is set ... `cedar_owner` bypasses the policy silently — no error, no warning — and `GET /clients` returns rows from every tenant

report_2.evidence: the app runs as the table-owning role, RLS is enabled but not forced, so Postgres exempts the owner from the `tenant_isolation` policy and every `/clients` call returns rows from all tenants. ... `cedarline/.env` sets `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://cedar_owner:ownerpw@localhost:5432/cedar`

report_1.evidence: Change `cedarline/.env` (and `.env.example`) so the running application's `DATABASE_URL` uses `cedar_app`, not `cedar_owner` ... Keep `cedar_owner` reserved exclusively for `cedarline/scripts/migrate.sh` ... one schema-owning role used only by migration tooling, one least-privilege, non-owning, non-bypass application role used for all runtime queries

report_2.evidence: Point the application's `DATABASE_URL` at the non-owning `cedar_app` role, not `cedar_owner`. `cedar_owner` should be reserved for running migrations/DDL only ... migrations should run as an owner/admin role, the API should run as a strictly-limited, non-owner application role

report_1.evidence: `cedarline/scripts/reset-local-db.sh` (the script used to "wipe and rebuild") only does: ... `docker compose down -v` ... It never re-runs `db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql`. Because `docker compose down -v` deletes the named volume ... the `cedar_app` role ... is destroyed and recreated from scratch. Migrations don't create `cedar_app`; only the bootstrap script does. ... a teammate must manually re-run the one-time bootstrap after any wipe

report_2.evidence: `docker compose up -d db` then reinitializes a brand-new cluster ... which only creates the role named in `POSTGRES_USER` (`cedar_owner` ...) ... it never runs `db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql`. ... that bootstrap script is explicitly "One-time bootstrap ... Not part of automatic database initialization; run by hand on first setup." ... the teammate has to manually re-run the bootstrap step
